title: The How Kenyan Creators Get Paid Internationally Setup That Started Generating Revenue
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: Kenyan creators face a common roadblock: getting paid internationally. Traditional payment gateways like PayPal and Stripe often exclude them due to geographic restrictions or stringent KYC requirements. This leaves talented digital creators—selling ebooks, courses, or art—stranded without a way to collect revenue from global buyers. One Nairobi-based graphic designer, Juma, found himself in this exact situation. Despite having a growing international client base, he couldn’t accept payments outside Kenya. His story, however, took a turn when he discovered a blockchain-based solution that removed these barriers entirely. Here’s how he did it.
The Situation Before the Solution

Juma, a graphic designer in Nairobi, had built a reputation for creating stunning digital templates. His work caught the attention of clients in Europe and the US, but he couldn’t invoice them directly. PayPal wasn’t available in Kenya for receiving international payments, and local banks charged exorbitant fees for wire transfers. Juma resorted to asking friends abroad to collect payments on his behalf, but this was unreliable and unprofessional. His revenue was capped by geography, and he felt trapped. Without a way to accept payments globally, his business couldn’t scale. He needed a solution that didn’t rely on traditional banking or payment processors.
Why Conventional Options Had Already Failed

Juma tried everything. He applied for a PayPal account but was rejected due to his location. Stripe required a US bank account, which he couldn’t open. Even local payment gateways like M-Pesa were limited to domestic transactions. Crypto exchanges seemed like an option, but they required KYC verification, which he wanted to avoid. Juma needed a system that was global, anonymous, and didn’t require a bank account. Conventional platforms were built for the West, not for creators in Kenya or other restricted regions. He realized he needed a solution designed specifically for his situation.
The Stack That Was Put Together

Juma discovered Unchained Commerce and implemented their Web3 Wallet Connect ($19.99) tool. This allowed him to accept payments in over 300 cryptocurrencies, including BTC, ETH, and USDT, directly on his website. The frontend integration generated multi-chain QR codes, making it easy for international buyers to pay. He paired this with the Core Shop Engine ($49.99) for automated delivery of his digital templates and PDF receipts. The stack required no payment processor account, no KYC, and no buyer account. It was fully automated, global, and worked instantly. Juma’s store was live within a week, ready to accept payments from anywhere in the world.
The blockchain has no map of restricted countries — a wallet in Lagos processes identically to one in London.
What Happened After Going Live

Within days of launching, Juma received his first international payment—0.02 BTC from a client in Germany. Over the next month, he closed deals with buyers in the UK, Canada, and Australia, all paying in their preferred cryptocurrencies. His revenue tripled, and he no longer relied on intermediaries. The automated delivery system saved him hours of manual work, and the PDF receipts added a professional touch. Juma’s business was no longer limited by geography. He could focus on creating, knowing his payment system worked seamlessly for anyone, anywhere.
The Lessons That Apply to Your Situation

Juma’s success proves that blockchain-based payments remove geographic barriers for creators in Kenya and beyond. By bypassing traditional payment processors, he gained access to a global market. The key is to use tools designed for restricted regions—no KYC, no bank accounts, and instant automation. If you’re a creator in Nigeria, Ghana, Pakistan, or any other blocked country, this same stack can work for you. The lesson? Don’t let payment limitations hold you back. There’s a solution that’s global, anonymous, and ready to scale with your business.
How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

Creators in Africa can sell digital products internationally by using blockchain-based payment systems. These platforms bypass geographic restrictions and banking requirements, allowing you to accept payments in cryptocurrencies like BTC, ETH, and USDT. Tools like Unchained Commerce’s Web3 Wallet Connect enable frontend wallet integration, generating multi-chain QR codes for easy transactions. This works for creators in Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, and beyond, removing the need for PayPal, Stripe, or bank accounts. The result? Global access to buyers without the usual barriers.
Creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh are already using this infrastructure to get paid globally.
Related: How Creators Like You Are Getting Paid

If you’re in Nigeria, Ghana, Pakistan, or another restricted country, the same principles apply. Unchained Commerce’s Web3 Wallet Connect ($19.99) lets you accept over 300 cryptocurrencies, ensuring buyers from anywhere can pay you instantly. Pair it with the Core Shop Engine for automated delivery, and you’ve got a full stack that works globally. Check out the setup at https://unchained-commerce.github.io/Shop/index.html and start selling without borders.
Your Action Checklist

Confirm which coins have the best P2P conversion options in your country before you configure your payment settings — USDT on Tron is a safe starting point for most African and South Asian markets. Create your NOWPayments account and add wallet addresses for your primary coins. Deploy the Core Shop Engine with your product files and NOWPayments credentials. Set up Web3 Wallet Connect so buyers can see live prices and QR codes for payment. Run a complete test transaction on mainnet and confirm delivery works end to end. Set up a P2P exchange account — Binance P2P, a local exchange, or both — so you have a conversion route ready the moment your first real sale comes in. Publish your store and share the URL with your existing audience. Track your first transactions in the Admin Dashboard and confirm the conversion rate you are getting from P2P exchange.
Geographic neutrality is not a feature of crypto payments — it is the fundamental property of how blockchains work.
Web3 Wallet Connect — $19.99. Frontend wallet integration with multi-chain QR generation and live price feeds. Let buyers pay in BTC, ETH, USDT, SOL, and 300 more coins.
